description: In Arabic, emphatic consonants (/sˤ, dˤ, tˤ, ɬˤ/) trigger pharyngealization (emphasis) on adjacent vowels, causing retraction and lowering of the following vowel (especially /a/ → [ɑ]). This coarticulatory effect spreads at least one syllable to the right, creating an emphatic allophonic domain.
sources: Al-Masri, A. (2005). _Pharyngealization in Arabic: An Acoustic Study_. Journal of Arabic Linguistics, 47, 1-24.
Khattab, G. (2008). _Phonetic and Phonological Aspects of Emphatic Consonants in Arabic_. In _Arabic Phonetics_ (pp. 31-50). Edinburgh University Press.
Zawaydeh, B. A. (1998). _The Phonology of Arabic: A Typological Study_. University of Texas at Austin dissertation.
